description: Capitation reweighting, access target, digital/urgent care and PHO accountability, without uncapped primary medical FFS.
Formula improves allocation but marginal activity remains weakly funded.
Eligible primary medical activity becomes demand-led through scheduled benefits, but place accountability remains weak.
Demand-led eligible medical activity plus capitation and explicit place/population responsibility.
Capitation + uncapped scheduled primary medical FFS + place accountability + urgent/ambulance alternatives + scope-enabled supply + strong data/audit/KPIs.
Activity becomes demand-led but controls, place accountability and equity protections are weak.
ACC activity payments are constrained without compensating Health NZ-funded upstream supply.
Urgent/ambulance alternatives are strengthened but the primary medical marginal payment signal remains weak.
Broader providers can generate some activity, but payment architecture and place accountability are not fully reformed.
Population accountability and outreach improve, but marginal activity funding is not materially uncapped.
